reasoning: Shantanu is one of us, I respect Thakurbari and what he means to our Matua people. But didi's card comes to my hand every month — real rice, real money for my daughter's education. My family lives on the edge; if the card stops, we go hungry. Shantanu says CAA will help, but I heard what happened in Assam, and I don't know if BJP far away in Delhi will reach my house the way didi has. I cannot vote against the one hand that feeds me.
primary_drivers: Direct welfare dependence — Khadya Sathi card is survival, not politics
Fear of losing schemes if government changes
Proven, consistent delivery by TMC vs. uncertain promises from BJP
